test_output_summary: 23 tests in tests/test_tool_types.py all pass (0.07s). Pre-existing failures in test_build_system.py::test_jarvis_version_command (env-shim 'No module named jarvis' under /usr/local/bin/python) and test_reserved_packages.py::TestAC002_EmptyNamespace[jarvis.tools] (cross-test pollution because importing jarvis.tools.types registers a 'types' attr on the jarvis.tools package — to be addressed by TASK-J002-015 which unseals the package and updates the reserved-empty assertion). Both failures are unrelated to this task's acceptance criteria and run-isolated tests/test_reserved_packages.py passes 56/56.
implementation_notes: Created src/jarvis/tools/types.py with three Pydantic v2 models per docs/design/FEAT-JARVIS-002/models/DM-tool-types.md and docs/architecture/decisions/ADR-ARCH-021-tools-return-structured-errors.md: WebResult (title/url min_length=1, snippet default '', score in [0,1]); CalendarEvent (id, title, start, end, optional location/description, with @model_validator(mode='after') asserting end >= start); DispatchError (Literal-typed category covering the six dispatch failure modes, detail, optional agent_id/tool_name, correlation_id, plus to_tool_string() rendering 'TIMEOUT: timeout — <detail>' for timeouts and 'ERROR: <category> — <detail>' otherwise per ADR-ARCH-021). All three set ConfigDict(extra='ignore'). Test file mirrors the four AC blocks with class-based organisation, parametrises non-timeout categories, asserts inclusive [0,1] score bounds and end==start equality, and verifies extra='ignore' both behaviourally (unknown kwarg dropped) and via model_config introspection.
concerns: Cross-test pollution: importing jarvis.tools.types from any test causes Python to register the 'types' submodule on the jarvis.tools package, which trips TASK-J001-010's TestAC002_EmptyNamespace[jarvis.tools] assertions. This is an expected feature-level conflict — FEAT-JARVIS-002 unseals jarvis.tools, and TASK-J002-015 (assemble_tool_list + tools/__init__) is the planned site to update the reserved-empty assertion. No action required from this task.
Pre-existing test_build_system.py::test_jarvis_version_command failure is an environment issue (test invokes /usr/local/bin/python which has no jarvis installed); unrelated to this task.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `src/jarvis/tools/types.py` defines `WebResult(BaseModel)` with `title: str (min_length=1)`, `url: str (min_length=1)`, `snippet: str = ""`, `score: float (ge=0, le=1) = 0.0`.
completion_promises.evidence: WebResult declared in src/jarvis/tools/types.py with title=Field(min_length=1), url=Field(min_length=1), snippet:str='', score=Field(default=0.0, ge=0.0, le=1.0). Tests assert empty title/url rejection, score < 0 and > 1 rejection, and inclusive boundaries 0.0 and 1.0 acceptance.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Defines `CalendarEvent(BaseModel)` with `id: str`, `title: str`, `start: datetime`, `end: datetime`, `location: str | None`, `description: str | None`, and a `@model_validator(mode="after")` asserting `end >= start`.
completion_promises.evidence: CalendarEvent declared in src/jarvis/tools/types.py with required id/title/start/end and optional location/description. _end_must_not_precede_start uses @model_validator(mode='after') and raises ValueError when end < start. Tests cover minimal/full construction, end==start acceptance, and end < start rejection via ValidationError.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Defines `DispatchError(BaseModel)` with `category: Literal["unresolved","invalid_payload","invalid_timeout","timeout","specialist_error","transport_stub"]`, `detail: str`, `agent_id: str | None`, `tool_name: str | None`, `correlation_id: str`, and `to_tool_string() -> str` method rendering `"ERROR: <category> — <detail>"` or `"TIMEOUT: ..."` per ADR-ARCH-021 conventions.
completion_promises.evidence: DispatchError declared in src/jarvis/tools/types.py with the six-member Literal category, detail, optional agent_id/tool_name, required correlation_id. to_tool_string() returns 'TIMEOUT: timeout — <detail>' for category=='timeout' and 'ERROR: <category> — <detail>' otherwise (em-dash separator per ADR-ARCH-021). Tests parametrise the five non-timeout categories asserting the ERROR prefix and dedicated test asserts the TIMEOUT prefix; invalid category strings are rejected by Pydantic validation.

Each of WebResult, CalendarEvent, DispatchError sets model_config = ConfigDict(extra='ignore'). TestExtraIgnore verifies this both behaviourally (unknown kwargs are silently dropped — no AttributeError but the attribute is not set) and via model_config['extra'] == 'ignore' introspection for all three classes.
